sections.paragraph:
προσπαίω 1 aor. προσέπαισα (Soph., Fgm. 311 [335 Pearson]; schol. on Aeschyl., Prom. 885; Ps. 90, 12 Sym.) strike, beat against τινί someth. Mt 7:25 cj. by Lachmann for προσέπεσαν; favored by SNaber, Mnemosyne 9, 1881, 276 and EbNestle, ZNW 9, 1908, 252f. B-D-F §202.—M-M.

aor. aor. = aorist

Soph Soph , V b.c.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

schol. schol. = scholion, scholia, scholiast

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Ps. Ps. = pseudo, pseudonymous

Sym. Sym. = Symmachus, Greek version of the OT—List 2, beg., 5

someth. someth. = something

  1. cj. = conjecture(d by)

Lachmann Lachmann = KLachmann. This abbreviation used when no other editors of the Gk. NT are mentioned.

ZNW ZNW = Zeitschrift für die ntl. Wissenschaft—List 6

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 884
